project: Native SQLite via KV Channel Protocol
description: Replace WASM-based SQLite with a native Rust SQLite binary (napi-rs addon) that routes page-level KV operations over a new WebSocket KV channel protocol. Includes WASM VFS improvements, protocol schema, native addon, server-side endpoints, integration, and concurrency/eviction hardening from adversarial review.

userStories.title: Create KV channel protocol BARE schema (v1.bare)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the KV channel protocol schema defined so that both client and server can serialize/deserialize messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Create directory engine/sdks/schemas/kv-channel-protocol/
Create v1.bare with all types from docs-internal/engine/NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'Schema' section (lines 86-233)

Types include: Id, ActorOpenRequest, ActorCloseRequest, ActorOpenResponse, ActorCloseResponse, KvKey, KvValue, KvGetRequest, KvPutRequest, KvDeleteRequest, KvDeleteRangeRequest, RequestData union, ErrorResponse, KvGetResponse, KvPutResponse, KvDeleteResponse, ResponseData union, ToServerRequest, ToServerPong, ToServer union, ToClientResponse, ToClientPing, ToClientClose, ToClient union
Schema follows same file structure as runner-protocol/v7.bare (with MARK comments for sections)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reference engine/sdks/schemas/runner-protocol/v7.bare for BARE schema conventions. The KV types mirror runner protocol KV types but omit KvListRequest, KvDropRequest, and KvMetadata.

userStories.title: Create KV channel protocol TypeScript versioned handler
userStories.description: As a developer, I need TypeScript code to handle versioned BARE serialization/deserialization of KV channel protocol messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Create engine/sdks/schemas/kv-channel-protocol/versioned.ts following the same pattern as runner-protocol's versioned TypeScript
Generate or hand-write TypeScript types from v1.bare schema
Export serialize/deserialize functions for ToServer and ToClient message types
Include protocol version constant (PROTOCOL_VERSION = 1)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the VBARE versioning pattern from engine/sdks/schemas/runner-protocol/. Check how runner-protocol/versioned.ts handles version embedding.

userStories.title: Add deleteRange to WASM VFS KvVfsOptions and migrate xTruncate/xDelete
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the WASM VFS to use deleteRange for efficient O(1) chunk deletion instead of enumerating individual chunk keys.
acceptanceCriteria: Add deleteRange(start: Uint8Array, end: Uint8Array): Promise<void> to KvVfsOptions interface in rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-vfs/src/types.ts
Update xTruncate in vfs.ts to use deleteRange instead of deleteBatch for chunks beyond truncation point
Update #delete in vfs.ts to use deleteRange instead of enumerating chunk keys
The range for deleteRange should cover all chunk keys for the file tag being deleted (chunk keys are lexicographically contiguous due to fixed prefix + big-endian chunk index)
Update all implementations of KvVfsOptions to include deleteRange (check rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/db/shared.ts createActorKvStore function)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md lines 436-446 for migration plan. The runner protocol already has KvDeleteRangeRequest. The VFS chunk keys use [SQLITE_PREFIX, SCHEMA_VERSION, CHUNK_PREFIX, FILE_TAG, chunk_index_u32_be] layout, so a range from chunk 0 to chunk MAX covers all chunks for a file tag.

userStories.title: Add PRAGMA settings to WASM VFS open method
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the WASM VFS to set PRAGMA busy_timeout and page_size after opening a database for KV-backed SQLite consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: After sqlite3.open_v2() in SqliteVfs.open() method in vfs.ts, execute PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000 and PRAGMA page_size = 4096
Add comment referencing native VFS (rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/src/vfs.rs) and spec doc for sync requirement
Remove or update the existing TODO comment about PRAGMA tuning
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md lines 414-429. busy_timeout=5000 matches the file-system driver. page_size=4096 matches CHUNK_SIZE. WAL mode is NOT enabled for KV-backed SQLite.

userStories.title: Create sqlite-native package scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the @rivetkit/sqlite-native package structure set up with Cargo.toml, package.json, build.rs, and platform-specific npm packages.
acceptanceCriteria: Create rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/ directory
Create Cargo.toml with napi-rs, libsqlite3-sys (bundled feature), tokio, tungstenite, serde, and bare-ts or serde-bare dependencies
Create package.json for @rivetkit/sqlite-native with napi-rs build scripts and optional dependencies on platform packages
Create build.rs for napi-rs
Create src/lib.rs with mod declarations for kv, protocol, channel, vfs (empty stub modules that compile)
Create src/kv.rs, src/protocol.rs, src/channel.rs, src/vfs.rs as empty modules with placeholder comments
Create npm/ directory with platform package.json files for: linux-x64-gnu, linux-arm64-gnu, darwin-x64, darwin-arm64, win32-x64-msvc
The crate must NOT be added to the root Cargo workspace (it has its own Cargo.toml with napi-rs deps)
cargo check passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'Rust Project Structure' section (lines 585-619). Follow napi-rs conventions. The crate statically links libsqlite3-sys with bundled feature. Platform packages follow @napi-rs/cli naming conventions.

userStories.title: Implement sqlite-native kv.rs (KV key layout)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Rust KV key layout module that produces byte-identical keys to the TypeScript implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement constants in kv.rs: CHUNK_SIZE=4096, SQLITE_PREFIX=0x08, SQLITE_SCHEMA_VERSION=0x01, META_PREFIX=0x00, CHUNK_PREFIX=0x01
Implement file tag constants: FILE_TAG_MAIN=0x00, FILE_TAG_JOURNAL=0x01, FILE_TAG_WAL=0x02, FILE_TAG_SHM=0x03
Implement get_meta_key(file_tag) -> [u8; 4] producing [SQLITE_PREFIX, SCHEMA_VERSION, META_PREFIX, file_tag]
Implement get_chunk_key(file_tag, chunk_index: u32) -> [u8; 8] producing [SQLITE_PREFIX, SCHEMA_VERSION, CHUNK_PREFIX, file_tag, chunk_index_bytes (big-endian)]
Add comments cross-referencing rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-vfs/src/kv.ts
Add #[cfg(test)] unit tests verifying byte-identical output to TypeScript implementation
cargo check passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'Key Layout' section (lines 388-406). Must produce byte-identical keys to kv.ts. Test with known inputs and compare against expected byte arrays from the TypeScript constants.

userStories.title: Implement sqlite-native protocol.rs (BARE serialization)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Rust types and BARE ser/de for all KV channel protocol messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Define Rust structs/enums for all v1.bare types: ToServer, ToClient, ToServerRequest, ToServerPong, ToClientResponse, ToClientPing, ToClientClose, RequestData, ResponseData, ErrorResponse, ActorOpenRequest, ActorCloseRequest, ActorOpenResponse, ActorCloseResponse, KvGetRequest, KvPutRequest, KvDeleteRequest, KvDeleteRangeRequest, KvGetResponse, KvPutResponse, KvDeleteResponse
Implement BARE serialization/deserialization for ToServer and ToClient unions
Use serde_bare or hand-written BARE encoding following the v1.bare schema exactly
Add round-trip tests for each message type
cargo check passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: See engine/sdks/schemas/kv-channel-protocol/v1.bare for the schema. BARE is a simple binary format. Consider using the bare-io or serde_bare crate, or hand-write the encoding if no suitable crate exists. Union types in BARE use a varint tag prefix.

userStories.title: Implement sqlite-native channel.rs (WebSocket KV channel client)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Rust WebSocket client that connects to the KV channel endpoint, sends requests, correlates responses by requestId, and reconnects on failure.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement KvChannel struct with connect() that opens WebSocket to ws://{endpoint}/kv/connect?token={token}&namespace={ns}&protocol_version=1
Implement send_request(actor_id, request_data) -> ResponseData that serializes ToServerRequest, sends it, and waits for ToClientResponse with matching requestId
Implement requestId counter (u32, incrementing per request, resets to 0 on reconnect)
Implement reconnect with exponential backoff: initial=1000ms, max=30000ms, multiplier=2, jitter=0-25%
On reconnect, re-send ActorOpenRequest for all actors that were open before disconnect
On disconnect, fail all in-flight requests with connection error (do not retry automatically)
Handle ToClientPing by responding with ToServerPong
Handle ToClientClose by triggering reconnect
Use tokio for async WebSocket I/O with tungstenite
30-second timeout per KV operation (KV_EXPIRE = 30_000ms)
cargo check passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'Reconnect Behavior' (lines 487-508) and 'In-Flight Requests on Reconnect' (lines 500-508). The reconnect strategy matches the runner protocol (engine/sdks/typescript/runner/src/utils.ts). One WebSocket per process, shared across all actors.

userStories.title: Implement sqlite-native vfs.rs (custom SQLite VFS)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a custom SQLite VFS in Rust that maps VFS callbacks to KV operations via the channel.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement SQLite VFS using libsqlite3-sys raw C API (sqlite3_vfs, sqlite3_io_methods, sqlite3_file)
xRead: batch-fetch chunk keys via KvGetRequest, copy data to output buffer, handle partial chunks and short reads
xWrite: prefetch partial chunks if needed, batch-write via KvPutRequest, update metadata if size changed
xTruncate: KvDeleteRangeRequest for chunks beyond truncation point, optional partial chunk rewrite, update metadata
xDelete: KvDeleteRangeRequest for all chunks of file tag, KvDeleteRequest for metadata key
xSync: flush dirty metadata via KvPutRequest
xFileSize: read metadata key via KvGetRequest
xAccess: check metadata key existence via KvGetRequest
xLock/xUnlock: no-op (single-writer enforced by channel lock)
xOpen: register VFS scoped to actor, load existing metadata or create new file
xClose: flush dirty metadata, cleanup
Apply PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000 and page_size=4096 after opening a database
Use same 4 KiB chunk layout and key encoding as kv.rs
cargo check passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'VFS Callbacks' table (lines 433-443) and 'Custom VFS' section (lines 385-458). Each actor gets its own VFS instance parameterized by actor ID. Must use KvDeleteRangeRequest (not enumerate chunks) for xTruncate and xDelete. Add comment noting end-to-end tests are in driver-test-suite.

userStories.title: Implement sqlite-native lib.rs (napi-rs entry point)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the napi-rs bindings that expose connect, openDatabase, query, execute, closeDatabase, and disconnect to JavaScript.
acceptanceCriteria: Export connect(config: {url, token?, namespace}) -> KvChannel via napi-rs
Export openDatabase(channel, actorId) -> NativeDatabase that sends ActorOpenRequest optimistically
Export execute(db, sql, params?) -> {changes} for write statements
Export query(db, sql, params?) -> {rows, columns} for read statements
Export closeDatabase(db) that sends ActorCloseRequest and releases resources
Export disconnect(channel) that closes the WebSocket connection
Use #[napi] attribute macros for all exported functions and types
Tokio runtime is initialized once per process for async WebSocket I/O
cargo check passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'JavaScript Bindings' section (lines 326-361). The API is minimal and synchronous from JS perspective (except connect/disconnect). SQLite operations block the N-API thread while waiting for KV responses via tokio.

userStories.title: Add KV channel WebSocket endpoint to manager router
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the local dev manager to serve the KV channel WebSocket endpoint so native SQLite can connect during development.
acceptanceCriteria: Add WebSocket upgrade route at GET /kv/connect in rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/manager/router.ts
Accept query params: token, namespace, protocol_version
Validate token against config.token (RIVET_TOKEN). Skip auth with warning if not set in dev mode
Validate protocol_version=1, reject with HTTP error if unsupported
Deserialize incoming WebSocket binary messages as ToServer using BARE
Handle ActorOpenRequest: acquire single-writer lock per actorId, reject with actor_locked if held by another connection
Handle ActorCloseRequest: release lock for actorId
Handle KvGetRequest: read from actor KV storage, return KvGetResponse
Handle KvPutRequest: write to actor KV storage, return KvPutResponse
Handle KvDeleteRequest: delete from actor KV storage, return KvDeleteResponse
Handle KvDeleteRangeRequest: range-delete from actor KV storage, return KvDeleteResponse
Return ErrorResponse with appropriate error codes (actor_not_open, actor_locked, actor_not_found, key_too_large, etc.)
Send ToClientPing every 3 seconds, close connection if no ToServerPong within 15 seconds
On WebSocket disconnect, release all locks held by the connection
Serialize responses as ToClient using BARE
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'Connection' (lines 70-74), 'Authentication' (lines 462-477), 'Single-Writer Lock Semantics' (lines 285-303), and 'Error Codes' (lines 248-264). The manager already has KV read endpoints at GET /actors/{actor_id}/kv/keys/{key} - reuse existing KV access patterns. KV limits from rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/drivers/file-system/kv-limits.ts must be enforced.

userStories.title: Create engine KV channel crate with WebSocket handler
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the production engine to serve the KV channel WebSocket endpoint for native SQLite in deployed environments.
acceptanceCriteria: Create new crate at engine/packages/kv-channel/ (or add to engine/packages/pegboard-runner/)
Add to workspace members in root Cargo.toml
Implement WebSocket handler at /kv/connect route
Accept query params: token, namespace, protocol_version
Validate token against auth.admin_token (RIVET__AUTH__ADMIN_TOKEN)
Validate protocol_version=1
Deserialize BARE messages (ToServer), serialize responses (ToClient)
Implement single-writer lock per actor (HashMap<ActorId, ConnectionId>)
Route KV operations to actor KV storage (reuse existing actor_kv module patterns from engine/packages/pegboard/src/actor_kv/mod.rs)
Enforce KV limits from engine/packages/pegboard/src/actor_kv/mod.rs

Handle all error codes from spec (actor_locked, actor_not_open, actor_not_found, key_too_large, value_too_large, payload_too_large, batch_too_large, storage_quota_exceeded, keys_values_length_mismatch, unauthorized, internal_error)
Ping every 3s, timeout if no pong within 15s (matching runner protocol defaults)
Send ToClientClose on graceful shutdown, finish in-flight responses before closing
Release all locks on WebSocket disconnect
cargo check passes
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'Authentication' (lines 462-477), 'Edge Cases' (lines 305-318), and 'KV Limits' (lines 266-281). Ping/pong intervals match runner_update_ping_interval_ms=3000 and runner_ping_timeout_ms=15000 from engine/packages/config/src/config/pegboard.rs.

userStories.title: Integrate native SQLite in database provider
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the database provider to automatically use native SQLite when available and fall back to WASM.
acceptanceCriteria: Add nativeSqliteAvailable() function that attempts to load the @rivetkit/sqlite-native .node addon and catches all failure modes (missing file, glibc mismatch, N-API version mismatch, corrupted binary)
In rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/db/mod.ts, add conditional path: if native available, use nativeSqlite.openDatabase(); else log warning via pino and fall back to WASM
The Database interface exposed to actors (c.db.query(), c.db.execute()) must be identical regardless of backend
Warning message: 'native SQLite not available, falling back to WebAssembly. run npm rebuild to install native bindings.'
The KV channel connection (nativeSqlite.connect()) should be initialized once per process during startup, not per actor
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'Integration with RivetKit' (lines 363-381). The KV channel is independent of the runner protocol and can be connected during process initialization. nativeSqliteAvailable() must try-catch the require() call, not just check file existence.

userStories.title: Update release scripts and version management for sqlite-native
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the release pipeline to correctly version and publish @rivetkit/sqlite-native and its platform packages.
acceptanceCriteria: Add entry to scripts/release/update_version.ts for rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/package.json
Add entry for rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/npm/*/package.json (platform packages)
Add entry for rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/Cargo.toml version field
Verify getRivetkitPackages() in scripts/release/sdk.ts picks up sqlite-native and platform packages, or add them explicitly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'Release Script Updates' (lines 668-706). The existing rivetkit-typescript/packages/*/package.json glob may cover the main package but npm/ subdirectories need explicit handling.

userStories.title: Add Rust VFS unit tests with mock WebSocket server
userStories.description: As a developer, I need unit tests for the native VFS to validate chunk mapping, key encoding, error handling, and reconnection without requiring a real server.
acceptanceCriteria: Add #[cfg(test)] module in rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/src/ (can be in vfs.rs or a dedicated test module)
Implement mock WebSocket server that runs in-process with in-memory KV store
Test xRead/xWrite correctly map byte ranges to 4 KiB KV chunks
Test chunk boundary handling (partial reads/writes spanning chunks)
Test xTruncate and xDelete produce correct KV delete ranges
Test metadata (file size) persistence via xSync/xClose
Test file tags (main, journal) key encoding
Test error handling (actor not open, actor locked)
Test optimistic open pipelining (open + immediate KV requests)
Test reconnection (mock server drops connection, client reconnects and re-opens actors)
Add comment noting end-to-end tests are in driver-test-suite (rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/driver-test-suite/)
cargo test passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: See NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'Testing' section (lines 548-579). Layer 1 tests only. Layer 2 (driver test suite) already exists and will test native SQLite transparently when the addon is available.

userStories.title: Use spawn_blocking for napi SQLite exports
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all napi SQLite exports (query, execute, exec) to use tokio spawn_blocking so they don't block the Node.js event loop.
acceptanceCriteria: Change query, execute, and exec functions in rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/src/lib.rs from sync napi functions to async napi functions (#[napi] pub async fn)
Wrap all SQLite work (prepare, bind, step, column reads) inside get_runtime().spawn_blocking(move || { ... }).await
VFS callbacks call Handle::block_on() from spawn_blocking threads (not tokio worker threads), which is safe
The Node.js main thread returns a Promise and is never blocked

Add a code comment above the spawn_blocking call documenting the 3 threading approaches considered (spawn_blocking, dedicated thread per actor, channel+block-in-place) and why spawn_blocking was chosen. Reference docs-internal/engine/NATIVE_SQLITE_REVIEW_FINDINGS.md Finding 1 for details.
Existing integration tests still pass (tests use #[test] not #[tokio::test], VFS callbacks use Handle::block_on from non-async context)
cargo check passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: See docs-internal/engine/NATIVE_SQLITE_REVIEW_FINDINGS.md Finding 1. SQLite's C API is synchronous (xRead must return bytes, not a future). spawn_blocking dispatches to tokio's blocking thread pool (default cap 512, separate from async worker pool). One blocking thread per concurrent actor, not per request. The WASM VFS avoids this via Asyncify (WASM-specific, not available for native code).

userStories.title: Unconditional lock eviction on ActorOpenRequest (engine)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the engine KV channel server to unconditionally transfer actor locks to the new connection on re-open, so reconnection doesn't fail with actor_locked.
acceptanceCriteria: In engine/packages/pegboard-kv-channel/src/lib.rs, change actor_locks type from HashMap<String, u64> to HashMap<String, (u64, Arc<Mutex<HashSet<String>>>)> where the HashSet is the connection's open_actors set
In handle_actor_open: if actor is locked by a different conn_id, remove the actor from the old connection's open_actors set via the stored Arc reference, then grant the lock to the new connection
The eviction must happen under the actor_locks mutex so there is no window where both connections pass the auth check
After eviction, the old connection's next KV request fails the fast-path check (open_actors.contains) immediately, then hits the slow path which returns actor_not_open
The global actor_locks mutex is never acquired on the KV hot path (only during open/close)
On disconnect cleanup, iterate the connection's open_actors and remove entries from actor_locks only if the lock is still held by this conn_id (another connection may have evicted it)
cargo check passes
userStories.notes: See docs-internal/engine/NATIVE_SQLITE_REVIEW_FINDINGS.md Finding 4. This is safe because: locks are in-memory only (no FDB state), old connection is dead or stale, old connection's in-flight FDB transactions complete normally, single-writer safety maintained by mutex atomicity. In multi-server deployments, locks are per-pod. Cross-process single-writer is enforced by actor scheduling, not KV channel locks.

userStories.title: Unconditional lock eviction on ActorOpenRequest (manager)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the manager KV channel handler to unconditionally transfer actor locks to the new connection on re-open.
acceptanceCriteria: In rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/manager/kv-channel.ts, update the lock acquisition logic in ActorOpenRequest handling
If the actor is locked by a different connection, transfer the lock to the new connection instead of returning actor_locked error
Remove the actor from the old connection's openActors set so the old connection's next KV request fails with actor_not_open

The old connection's openActors must be accessible from the lock map. Store a reference to the connection's openActors Set in the lock entry (e.g., Map<string, { connId: number, openActors: Set<string> }>)
On disconnect cleanup, only release locks still held by this connection (check connId matches before deleting)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See docs-internal/engine/NATIVE_SQLITE_REVIEW_FINDINGS.md Finding 4. Mirror the same eviction logic as US-017 but in TypeScript for the manager. The manager runs single-process so no multi-server concerns.

userStories.title: Wait for ActorOpenResponse on reconnect (client)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the native SQLite client to wait for ActorOpenResponse before sending KV requests on reconnect, to avoid racing with lock eviction.
acceptanceCriteria: In rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/src/channel.rs, update the reconnect logic to wait for each ActorOpenResponse before marking the actor as ready for KV requests
The initial open (first connection, not a reconnect) can remain optimistic (send ActorOpenRequest + KV requests without waiting)
Add a per-actor ready flag or barrier that KV requests check. On reconnect, KV requests for an actor block until its ActorOpenResponse is received
If ActorOpenResponse returns an error, fail all pending KV requests for that actor
Existing optimistic pipelining test still passes for the initial connection case
Add a test verifying that on reconnect, KV requests wait for the re-open response
cargo check passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: See docs-internal/engine/NATIVE_SQLITE_REVIEW_FINDINGS.md Finding 4 'Client-side change' section. This prevents a race where the client sends KV requests before the server has processed the re-open and transferred the lock. The initial open remains optimistic because there's no stale lock to evict on first connection.

userStories.title: Per-actor channel routing in engine KV channel server
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the engine KV channel to process requests for different actors concurrently instead of sequentially.
acceptanceCriteria: In engine/packages/pegboard-kv-channel/src/lib.rs, add a HashMap<String, mpsc::Sender<Request>> that routes incoming requests to per-actor channels
Each actor gets a spawned tokio task that drains its channel sequentially (preserving intra-actor ordering)
When a request arrives, route it to the actor's channel (create the channel and task on first request for that actor)
Cross-actor requests execute concurrently. Actor A's slow KvPutRequest no longer blocks Actor B's KvGetRequest
Do NOT use tokio::spawn per request (breaks optimistic pipelining ordering guarantee and journal write ordering)
Clean up per-actor tasks and channels when the connection disconnects or the actor is closed
cargo check passes
userStories.notes: See docs-internal/engine/NATIVE_SQLITE_REVIEW_FINDINGS.md Finding 2. A single actor never has multiple concurrent KV requests (VFS callbacks are sequential within SQLite, and connections are single-threaded). Per-actor sequential processing matches the client's behavior exactly. Tokio tasks are lightweight (few hundred bytes each). This only matters after US-016 is implemented (with sync napi exports, only one actor does VFS operations at a time).

userStories.title: Per-actor channel routing in runner KV handling
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the runner protocol's KV handling to process requests for different actors concurrently.
acceptanceCriteria: In engine/packages/pegboard-runner/src/ws_to_tunnel_task.rs, apply the same per-actor channel routing pattern as US-020
Add HashMap<String, mpsc::Sender<Request>> for routing KV requests to per-actor tasks
Each actor's task drains its channel sequentially (preserving intra-actor ordering)
Cross-actor KV requests execute concurrently
Do NOT use tokio::spawn per request (breaks ordering guarantees)
Clean up per-actor tasks on connection disconnect
cargo check passes
userStories.notes: See docs-internal/engine/NATIVE_SQLITE_REVIEW_FINDINGS.md Finding 2 TODO section. The runner protocol (ws_to_tunnel_task.rs lines 68-77) has the same sequential message processing pattern as the KV channel. Same fix, same rationale.

userStories.title: Update spec with review findings
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the main spec document updated with the threading model, lock eviction semantics, and multi-server notes from the adversarial review.
acceptanceCriteria: In docs-internal/engine/NATIVE_SQLITE_DATA_CHANNEL.md 'JavaScript Bindings' section, change TypeScript signatures to return Promise<...> instead of sync values

Add note in JavaScript Bindings: 'SQLite operations run on tokio's blocking thread pool via spawn_blocking. VFS callbacks call Handle::block_on() from blocking threads (not tokio worker threads). The Node.js main thread is never blocked.'
In 'Single-Writer Lock Semantics' section, add paragraph about unconditional lock eviction on ActorOpenRequest from a different connection
Add paragraph about multi-server deployments: locks are per-server-instance, cross-process single-writer enforced by actor scheduling
In 'Reconnect Behavior' section, add: 'On reconnect, the client must wait for ActorOpenResponse before sending KV requests for each actor. The initial open (first connection) can remain optimistic.'
No typecheck needed (markdown file)
userStories.notes: See docs-internal/engine/NATIVE_SQLITE_REVIEW_FINDINGS.md for the exact wording of all spec updates needed. Finding 1 requires async API signatures. Finding 4 requires eviction semantics, multi-server notes, and reconnect wait requirement.

userStories.title: Fix native SQLite onDisconnect DB writes test flake
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the native SQLite driver's 'completes onDisconnect DB writes before sleeping' test to pass reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: The test 'Actor Database (raw) Tests > completes onDisconnect DB writes before sleeping' passes for driver-file-system-native-sqlite
The test configures a disconnect insert with 250ms delay, waits SLEEP_WAIT_MS + 250ms, then expects getDisconnectInsertCount() == 1. Currently returns 0 on native SQLite.
Investigate whether the native SQLite addon's async lifecycle (spawn_blocking, VFS block_on) introduces a timing gap that causes the disconnect write to be lost or delayed

Fix the root cause. Possible causes: native SQLite close/flush not completing before sleep, race between onDisconnect callback and actor sleep, or the native db provider cleanup not awaiting pending writes
All other driver-file-system-native-sqlite database tests continue to pass
cargo check passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/ (if Rust changes needed)
userStories.notes: The same test passes on the WASM VFS (driver-file-system). The failure is native-sqlite-specific. The test fixture actor (actor-db.ts) calls configureDisconnectInsert(true, 250) which sets up an onDisconnect handler that does a DB write after 250ms delay. The native SQLite path may not be flushing or awaiting writes correctly during actor sleep/disconnect lifecycle.

userStories.title: Fix metadata encoding mismatch between WASM and native VFS
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the WASM and native VFS file metadata encoding to be byte-identical so actors can switch backends without data corruption.
acceptanceCriteria: WASM VFS (sqlite-vfs/src/vfs.ts) and native VFS (sqlite-native/src/vfs.rs) produce identical metadata byte layouts

Currently WASM uses FILE_META_VERSIONED.serializeWithEmbeddedVersion() producing 10 bytes (2-byte u16 LE version + 8-byte u64 LE size), while native uses 9 bytes (1-byte u8 version + 8-byte u64 LE size). One must be updated to match the other.
Align the native VFS to match the WASM VFS encoding (2-byte u16 LE version prefix from vbare), since the WASM VFS is the existing production format
Update native VFS encode_file_meta and decode_file_meta to use 2-byte u16 LE version prefix
Update native VFS unit tests to reflect the correct byte layout
cargo test passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CRITICAL data compatibility issue. The vbare library's serializeWithEmbeddedVersion prepends a 2-byte u16 LE version. The native VFS uses a 1-byte u8 version. If an actor is created with WASM VFS and later accessed via native VFS (or vice versa), metadata deserialization will fail silently or produce wrong file sizes. The WASM encoding is the production format, so native must align to it.

userStories.title: Add cross-backend VFS compatibility driver tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need driver tests that verify data written by one VFS backend can be read by the other.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a new test file in rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/driver-test-suite/tests/ for cross-backend VFS compatibility
Test 1 (WASM-to-native): Create actor with WASM VFS, write data to DB, sleep the actor, recreate with native SQLite VFS, read and verify data matches
Test 2 (native-to-WASM): Create actor with native SQLite VFS, write data to DB, sleep the actor, recreate with WASM VFS, read and verify data matches
Tests should use the driver-file-system and driver-file-system-native-sqlite test configurations
Both tests pass when native SQLite addon is available
Tests are skipped gracefully when native SQLite addon is not available
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These tests validate US-024 (metadata encoding fix) end-to-end. The tests need two different driver configurations: one forcing WASM VFS and one forcing native SQLite. The actor should write structured data (multiple rows, various types) to exercise chunk boundaries. Sleep/recreate cycle ensures data is flushed through KV and re-read from scratch.

userStories.title: Wrap VFS callbacks in catch_unwind to prevent UB on panic
userStories.description: As a developer, I need VFS callbacks to never unwind through C code, which is undefined behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: All extern 'C' VFS callback functions in sqlite-native/src/vfs.rs are wrapped in std::panic::catch_unwind
On panic, callbacks return SQLITE_IOERR (or appropriate SQLite error code) instead of unwinding
Add a helper macro or function like vfs_callback_guard that wraps the callback body, catches panics, and returns the error code
Log caught panics via tracing::error with the panic message
Existing tests still pass
cargo check passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: There are ~20 VFS callbacks that are extern C functions. Panics from slice::from_raw_parts, HashMap operations, or arithmetic can unwind through SQLite's C stack frames, which is UB. catch_unwind converts panics to a Result, then we return SQLITE_IOERR. Use AssertUnwindSafe wrapper since the callback closures capture mutable state.

userStories.title: Use constant-time token comparison in guard KV channel routing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the KV channel auth token comparison to use constant-time comparison to prevent timing attacks.
acceptanceCriteria: In engine/packages/guard/src/routing/kv_channel.rs, replace the != token comparison with a constant-time comparison
Use the subtle crate's ConstantTimeEq trait or equivalent
Add subtle as a workspace dependency if not already present
Also fix the same issue in engine/packages/guard/src/routing/runner.rs for consistency
cargo check passes
userStories.notes: Both kv_channel.rs and runner.rs use regular != for token comparison. The TypeScript side already uses timingSafeEqual. The subtle crate is the standard Rust crate for constant-time operations.

userStories.title: Stop leaking internal error details to KV channel clients
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the KV channel server to return generic error messages instead of exposing internal error details.
acceptanceCriteria: In engine/packages/pegboard-kv-channel/src/lib.rs, all error_response calls that pass internal error messages (err.to_string()) are changed to pass generic messages
Internal errors should log the full error via tracing::error with structured fields, then return a generic message like 'internal error' to the client
Specific, known error codes (actor_not_open, actor_locked, etc.) continue to return descriptive messages
Only internal_error responses get the generic treatment
cargo check passes
userStories.notes: Lines 571, 578, 648, 687, 692, 728, 733, 756 in lib.rs currently pass err.to_string() which can expose stack traces, database error messages, and internal state. Log full errors server-side, return sanitized messages to clients.

userStories.title: Replace string matching with concrete KV error types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need typed KV error variants instead of string matching on error messages for error classification.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a KV error enum in engine/packages/pegboard/src/ using the existing RivetError derive macro pattern
Add variants for: StorageQuotaExceeded (with remaining/payload size metadata), and any other classifiable KV errors
Update actor_kv::put() and related functions in engine/packages/pegboard/src/actor_kv/ to return the concrete error type instead of anyhow strings
Update engine/packages/pegboard-kv-channel/src/lib.rs to match on error variants instead of msg.contains('not enough space left in storage')
Update the TypeScript manager kv-channel.ts to use the same pattern (match on error type/code, not string contents)
cargo check passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Currently only one location does string matching (storage quota in handle_kv_put). The codebase already has #[derive(RivetError)] infrastructure in engine/packages/error/. The existing pegboard errors at engine/packages/pegboard/src/errors.rs show the pattern. actor_kv/utils.rs line 79 defines the storage error as a format! string.

userStories.title: Fix KV channel singleton lifecycle and disconnect cleanup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the native SQLite KV channel to be properly disconnected on process shutdown and recover from dead connections.
acceptanceCriteria: In rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/db/native-sqlite.ts, ensure disconnect() is called during actor instance cleanup or process shutdown
Register a process shutdown handler (or hook into existing RivetKit shutdown) that calls kvChannel.disconnect()
Add health check: if the singleton kvChannel becomes unhealthy (WebSocket dies permanently), new database instances should recreate the channel instead of using the dead handle
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Currently the singleton kvChannel at native-sqlite.ts:62 is created once and never cleaned up. The disconnect() function is exported but never called anywhere. On process shutdown, the WebSocket connection leaks without graceful close.

userStories.title: Fix race condition in KV channel ping/pong and connection cleanup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the manager KV channel to handle concurrent connection state changes safely.
acceptanceCriteria: In rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/manager/kv-channel.ts, fix the ping/pong race condition where conn.ws could become null between the check and the send
In cleanupConnection, set conn.ws = null after cleanup to prevent use-after-close
Add null checks before all conn.ws.send() and conn.ws.close() calls
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In startPingPong (lines 84-105), between checking conn.closed and sending on conn.ws, the connection could be closed by onClose/onError handlers. cleanupConnection sets conn.closed = true but does NOT set conn.ws = null, leaving a stale reference.

userStories.title: Add bounded capacity and TTL cleanup for KV channel lock tables
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the KV channel lock tables to not grow unbounded when clients disconnect without cleanup.
acceptanceCriteria: In engine/packages/pegboard-kv-channel/src/lib.rs, add a maximum actor limit per connection (e.g., 1000 actors) and reject ActorOpenRequest with an error if exceeded
In rivetkit-typescript/packages/rivetkit/src/manager/kv-channel.ts, add periodic cleanup of stale lock entries where the owning connection is no longer alive
Add a sweep interval (e.g., every 60s) that iterates actorLocks and removes entries for dead connections
cargo check passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Engine side: actor_channels HashMap is unbounded, malicious client could exhaust memory. Manager side: if client disconnects without ActorCloseRequest, lock entries persist in actorLocks indefinitely until another connection opens the same actor.

userStories.title: Propagate metadata write failures in native VFS
userStories.description: As a developer, I need metadata write failures in the native VFS to be reported to SQLite instead of silently ignored.
acceptanceCriteria: In sqlite-native/src/vfs.rs, all metadata kv_put calls that currently ignore errors should propagate failures as SQLITE_IOERR
Affected locations: xClose (meta flush), xSync (meta flush), xTruncate (meta update), xWrite (meta update)
If metadata write fails during xClose, log the error via tracing::error and return SQLITE_IOERR
If metadata write fails during xSync, return SQLITE_IOERR so SQLite knows the sync failed
Existing tests still pass
cargo check passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: Currently in vfs.rs lines 185-195, 384-395, 415-424, 472-481, metadata writes can fail but errors are silently ignored. The meta_dirty flag may be left in an inconsistent state. On close, if metadata wasn't persisted, the file size is lost.

userStories.title: Replace eprintln with tracing in native SQLite addon
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all logging in the native SQLite addon to use the tracing crate for structured logging.
acceptanceCriteria: In rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/src/channel.rs, replace all eprintln! calls with tracing::warn! or tracing::error! with structured fields
In rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/src/lib.rs, replace any eprintln! calls with tracing equivalents
Add tracing as a dependency in rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/Cargo.toml if not already present
Initialize a tracing subscriber in the native addon's runtime initialization (or document that the host process must initialize tracing)
Log messages are lowercase per CLAUDE.md conventions
Update CLAUDE.md to add guidance that all Rust code must use tracing for logging, never eprintln/println
cargo check passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: Multiple eprintln! calls in channel.rs (line 346, 447-450, 496) and potentially lib.rs violate CLAUDE.md structured logging requirements. CLAUDE.md should be updated to explicitly state: never use eprintln/println for logging in Rust code, always use tracing.

userStories.title: Add prepared statement caching in native SQLite addon
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the native SQLite addon to cache prepared statements to avoid re-parsing the same SQL on every call.
acceptanceCriteria: In rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/src/lib.rs, add an LRU cache for prepared statements keyed by SQL string
Cache size should be configurable with a reasonable default (e.g., 64 or 128 entries)
query() and execute() check the cache before calling sqlite3_prepare_v2
Cached statements are reset (sqlite3_reset + sqlite3_clear_bindings) before reuse
Statements are finalized when evicted from cache or when database is closed
Add a test verifying that repeated queries reuse cached statements
cargo check passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: Currently every query/execute call in lib.rs re-parses SQL via sqlite3_prepare_v2. For actors with repeated queries (common pattern), this adds unnecessary overhead. The cache should be on the Rust side since that's where sqlite3_prepare_v2 is called. Use an LRU cache (lru crate) to bound memory.

userStories.title: URL-encode token in native SQLite WebSocket connection URL
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the WebSocket connection URL to properly encode the auth token to prevent URL parsing issues.
acceptanceCriteria: In rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/src/channel.rs, URL-encode the token parameter when constructing the WebSocket URL
Use the urlencoding crate or percent-encode the token value
Add urlencoding as a dependency if needed
Tokens containing special characters (& = ? #) are properly escaped
cargo check passes from within rivetkit-typescript/packages/sqlite-native/
userStories.notes: channel.rs lines 530-531 construct the WebSocket URL by string interpolation without encoding. If the token contains URL-special characters, the URL breaks.

userStories.title: Fix connection ID overflow in engine KV channel
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the connection ID counter to handle overflow safely instead of wrapping around.
acceptanceCriteria: In engine/packages/pegboard-kv-channel/src/lib.rs, change the AtomicU64 connection ID counter to use checked_add or wrapping_add with explicit handling
If the counter wraps (extremely unlikely but technically possible), log a warning and continue with the wrapped value
Alternatively, use a UUID for connection IDs instead of a sequential counter to eliminate the overflow concern entirely
cargo check passes
userStories.notes: next_conn_id uses AtomicU64::fetch_add(1, Relaxed) which wraps on overflow after ~18 billion connections. If it wraps, two connections could have the same ID, causing lock table corruption. Using UUID eliminates this entirely.
